vocabulary.example: Please send the invoice by Friday.
The customer requested a refund for the damaged item.
Please confirm your appointment by email.
The meeting room is available after 3 p.m.
All employees must submit the form today.
The deadline for the report is next Monday.
Please contact the sales department.
Each employee received a new ID card.
The office equipment will be replaced next week.
The schedule has changed because of the delay.
I have an appointment with the manager at 10 a.m.
The reservation was made under your name.
Keep the receipt for your expense report.
Travel expenses must be approved in advance.
The company updated its travel policy.
The candidate arrived early for the interview.
The interview will begin at 2 p.m.
New staff must complete safety training.
The purchase was approved by the supervisor.
The supplier delivered the items this morning.
The shipment is expected to arrive tomorrow.
There was a delay because of bad weather.
The item is currently out of stock.
Please send your request to the support team.
The manager will approve the budget tomorrow.
I attached the file to this email.
We will update the schedule this afternoon.
The company will announce the new policy soon.
The printer needs repair.
We need to replace the broken chair.
The team maintains the equipment every month.
The new location is convenient for customers.
We need a reliable assistant for this project.
The new system is more efficient than the old one.
The annual report will be published in June.
The monthly meeting is held on the first Monday.
Please review the contract before signing it.
The new branch will open next month.
The customer asked about the warranty.
The client requested a revised estimate.
My colleague will attend the meeting instead.
Please ask your supervisor for approval.
Employees receive health benefits.
The salary will be paid at the end of the month.
The project is within the budget.
The repair company sent an estimate.
There is no cancellation fee.
The delivery charge is included in the total.
Members receive a 10 percent discount.
The product comes with a one-year warranty.
Please read the instructions before using the device.
Conference registration closes on Friday.
Attendance at the training session is required.
The conference will be held in September.
Her presentation was clear and useful.
The facility is closed for maintenance.
Security staff checked all visitor passes.
Only managers have access to this file.
Please create an account before ordering.
Do not share your password with anyone.
Please contact support if the issue continues.
The supervisor will review the report.
We can arrange transportation for guests.
The store will deliver the package tomorrow.
Please notify us of any changes.
The event was postponed until next week.
You can cancel the reservation online.
The position requires two years of experience.
The price includes breakfast.
Please prepare the documents for the meeting.

grammar.prompt: Please ___ the form by Friday.
grammar.explanationZh: Please 後面接原形動詞，所以答案是 submit。
grammar.explanationJa: please の後ろは動詞の原形です。

grammar.prompt: The meeting has been ___ until next Monday.
grammar.explanationZh: has been 後面接過去分詞，表示被延期。
grammar.explanationJa: has been + 過去分詞で受け身です。

grammar.prompt: All employees must wear their ID badges ___ the office.
grammar.explanationZh: during 後面接名詞，during the office 表示在辦公室期間。
grammar.explanationJa: during の後ろは名詞です。

grammar.prompt: The manager is looking for a ___ assistant.
grammar.explanationZh: assistant 前面需要形容詞，reliable 是可靠的。
grammar.explanationJa: 名詞 assistant を修飾する形容詞が必要です。

grammar.prompt: If you have any questions, please contact the HR ___.
grammar.explanationZh: HR 後面需要名詞 department，表示人事部。
grammar.explanationJa: HR department で人事部です。

grammar.prompt: The new printer is ___ than the old one.
grammar.explanationZh: than 出現時通常用比較級，more efficient。
grammar.explanationJa: than があるので比較級です。

grammar.prompt: We need to finish the report ___ the deadline.
grammar.explanationZh: by the deadline 表示不遲於截止日前。
grammar.explanationJa: by は期限までに、という意味です。

grammar.prompt: The company will provide additional training ___ next month.
grammar.explanationZh: beginning next month 表示從下個月開始。
grammar.explanationJa: beginning next month で来月からです。

grammar.prompt: The package ___ yesterday afternoon.
grammar.explanationZh: yesterday 是過去時間，所以用過去式 arrived。
grammar.explanationJa: yesterday があるので過去形です。

grammar.prompt: The repair work will be completed ___ Friday.
grammar.explanationZh: by Friday 是星期五前完成。
grammar.explanationJa: by Friday は金曜日までにです。

grammar.prompt: The office is closed ___ the national holiday.
grammar.explanationZh: due to 後面接名詞，表示因為國定假日。
grammar.explanationJa: due to + 名詞で理由を表します。

grammar.prompt: Please send the file to Ms. Sato ___ possible.
grammar.explanationZh: as soon as possible 是固定說法，表示盡快。
grammar.explanationJa: as soon as possible はできるだけ早くです。

grammar.prompt: The training session is open to all ___ employees.
grammar.explanationZh: interested employees 表示有興趣的員工。
grammar.explanationJa: employees を修飾する形容詞 interested が必要です。

grammar.prompt: The store offers free delivery ___ orders over 5,000 yen.
grammar.explanationZh: on orders over 金額 是常見搭配，表示對某類訂單。
grammar.explanationJa: on orders over ... でその金額以上の注文に対してです。

grammar.prompt: The supervisor ___ the budget every month.
grammar.explanationZh: 主詞 supervisor 是第三人稱單數，一般現在式加 s。
grammar.explanationJa: 三人称単数なので reviews です。

grammar.prompt: The new software is easy ___ use.
grammar.explanationZh: easy to use 是固定結構。
grammar.explanationJa: easy to use で使いやすいです。

grammar.prompt: The employee ___ prepared the report is on vacation.
grammar.explanationZh: 先行詞是人 employee，所以用 who。
grammar.explanationJa: 人を説明する関係代名詞は who です。

grammar.prompt: This is the form ___ you need to complete.
grammar.explanationZh: 先行詞是物 form，可以用 that。
grammar.explanationJa: 物を説明する関係代名詞として that が使えます。

grammar.prompt: The restaurant is ___ to the station than before.
grammar.explanationZh: than before 需要比較級 closer。
grammar.explanationJa: than があるので closer です。

grammar.prompt: Please read the instructions ___ using the device.
grammar.explanationZh: before using 表示使用之前。
grammar.explanationJa: before + 動名詞で、する前にです。

grammar.prompt: The report was ___ by the accounting team.
grammar.explanationZh: was + 過去分詞是被動語態。
grammar.explanationJa: was + 過去分詞で受け身です。

grammar.prompt: Customers can return items ___ 30 days.
grammar.explanationZh: within 30 days 表示 30 天內。
grammar.explanationJa: within 30 days は30日以内です。

grammar.prompt: The team worked ___ to finish the project.
grammar.explanationZh: 修飾動詞 worked 要用副詞 quickly。
grammar.explanationJa: 動詞 worked を修飾する副詞が必要です。

grammar.prompt: The meeting will start ___ 9 a.m.
grammar.explanationZh: 具體時間點用 at。
grammar.explanationJa: 時刻には at を使います。

grammar.prompt: The manager asked ___ the invoice had been sent.
grammar.explanationZh: asked whether 表示詢問是否。
grammar.explanationJa: whether は「かどうか」です。

grammar.prompt: The conference room ___ cleaned every morning.
grammar.explanationZh: room 是單數，且被清潔，用 is cleaned。
grammar.explanationJa: 単数なので is cleaned です。

grammar.prompt: We are looking for someone ___ can work on weekends.
grammar.explanationZh: someone 是人，用 who。
grammar.explanationJa: 人なので who を使います。

grammar.prompt: This year's sales are ___ than last year's.
grammar.explanationZh: than last year's 需要比較級 higher。
grammar.explanationJa: 比較なので higher です。

grammar.prompt: The document is ___ in the shared folder.
grammar.explanationZh: be 動詞後面需要形容詞 available。
grammar.explanationJa: be 動詞の後ろは形容詞 available です。

grammar.prompt: The store will be closed ___ renovations.
grammar.explanationZh: due to renovations 表示因翻修而關閉。
grammar.explanationJa: due to + 名詞で理由です。

grammar.prompt: The staff meeting ___ at 10 a.m. every Tuesday.
grammar.explanationZh: 會議是被舉行，用 is held。
grammar.explanationJa: meeting は開催される側なので is held です。

grammar.prompt: Please call us ___ you arrive at the station.
grammar.explanationZh: when you arrive 表示你到達時。
grammar.explanationJa: when は「時」を表します。

grammar.prompt: The printer ___ is near the entrance is not working.
grammar.explanationZh: 先行詞 printer 是物，用 that。
grammar.explanationJa: 物なので that が使えます。

grammar.prompt: This manual is ___ than the previous version.
grammar.explanationZh: than 表示比較，用 clearer。
grammar.explanationJa: than があるので比較級です。

grammar.prompt: The guest made a reservation ___ the phone.
grammar.explanationZh: on the phone 表示透過電話。
grammar.explanationJa: on the phone で電話で、という意味です。

grammar.prompt: The office assistant answered the call ___.
grammar.explanationZh: 修飾 answered 要用副詞 politely。
grammar.explanationJa: 動詞 answered を修飾する副詞です。

grammar.prompt: The applicant ___ experience matches the job was selected.
grammar.explanationZh: whose 表示某人的，whose experience 是他的經驗。
grammar.explanationJa: whose は所有を表します。

grammar.prompt: The delivery was delayed ___ the road was closed.
grammar.explanationZh: because 後面接完整句子，說明原因。
grammar.explanationJa: because の後ろは文です。

grammar.prompt: The new policy will take effect ___ July 1.
grammar.explanationZh: 具體日期用 on。
grammar.explanationJa: 日付には on を使います。

grammar.prompt: The product was designed to operate ___.
grammar.explanationZh: 修飾 operate 用副詞 automatically。
grammar.explanationJa: 動詞 operate を修飾する副詞です。

grammar.prompt: The technician ___ the machine before the office opened.
grammar.explanationZh: before the office opened 是過去時間脈絡，用 inspected。
grammar.explanationJa: 過去の出来事なので inspected です。

grammar.prompt: This is the most ___ solution for the small team.
grammar.explanationZh: solution 前需要形容詞 practical。
grammar.explanationJa: 名詞 solution を修飾する形容詞です。

grammar.prompt: Please keep the receipt ___ you need to request a refund.
grammar.explanationZh: in case 表示以防萬一。
grammar.explanationJa: in case は念のため、万一に備えてです。

grammar.prompt: The branch office is located ___ the second floor.
grammar.explanationZh: 樓層用 on the second floor。
grammar.explanationJa: 階には on を使います。

grammar.prompt: The package ___ by the supplier tomorrow morning.
grammar.explanationZh: package 是被配送，所以用 will be delivered。
grammar.explanationJa: 未来の受け身です。

grammar.prompt: The hotel is ___ from the airport than the station.
grammar.explanationZh: than 表示比較，用 farther。
grammar.explanationJa: 比較級 farther です。

grammar.prompt: The team discussed the issue ___ the weekly meeting.
grammar.explanationZh: during 後接名詞 meeting。
grammar.explanationJa: during + 名詞です。

grammar.prompt: The employee ___ handled the complaint received praise.
grammar.explanationZh: employee 是人，用 who。
grammar.explanationJa: 人を説明するので who です。

grammar.prompt: The form must be filled out ___ before submission.
grammar.explanationZh: 修飾 filled out 要用副詞 completely。
grammar.explanationJa: 動詞句を修飾する副詞です。

grammar.prompt: The office will remain open ___ the renovation work continues.
grammar.explanationZh: although 後接句子，表示雖然。
grammar.explanationJa: although の後ろは文です。

grammar.prompt: The schedule ___ updated if the weather changes.
grammar.explanationZh: if 子句描述未來可能，主句用 will be updated。
grammar.explanationJa: 未来の受け身です。

grammar.prompt: The service desk is open ___ 9 a.m. to 6 p.m.
grammar.explanationZh: from A to B 是從 A 到 B。
grammar.explanationJa: from A to B の形です。

grammar.prompt: The new checklist is ___ useful for new staff.
grammar.explanationZh: 修飾形容詞 useful 要用副詞 especially。
grammar.explanationJa: 形容詞 useful を強める副詞です。

grammar.prompt: The office ordered chairs ___ are easier to move.
grammar.explanationZh: chairs 是物，用 that。
grammar.explanationJa: 物を説明する that です。

grammar.prompt: The latest version is ___ stable than the old one.
grammar.explanationZh: stable 的比較級是 more stable。
grammar.explanationJa: more stable で、より安定しているです。

grammar.prompt: Please notify the team ___ the shipment arrives.
grammar.explanationZh: as soon as 後接句子，表示一...就...。
grammar.explanationJa: as soon as は、するとすぐにです。

reading.passage: Dear Mr. Tanaka, Thank you for your order. One item is currently unavailable. We expect to receive more stock next week. If you prefer not to wait, we can issue a refund for that item.

questions.explanationZh: 文章說其中一件商品 currently unavailable，所以是通知商品問題。
questions.explanationJa: 商品が在庫切れのため連絡しています。

questions.prompt: What can Mr. Tanaka choose to receive?
questions.explanationZh: 文中寫 can issue a refund。
questions.explanationJa: refund と書かれています。

reading.passage: Notice: The third-floor conference room will be closed on Friday for equipment maintenance. Meetings scheduled in that room will be moved to Room 205. Please check the updated schedule before Friday morning.

questions.prompt: Why will the conference room be closed?
questions.explanationZh: notice 說 closed for equipment maintenance。
questions.explanationJa: 設備メンテナンスのため閉鎖です。

reading.passage: Hello Team, The monthly sales meeting has been moved from Tuesday to Wednesday at 10 a.m. Please bring your updated sales numbers and a short list of client questions.

questions.prompt: When will the meeting be held?
questions.explanationZh: meeting moved to Wednesday at 10 a.m.。
questions.explanationJa: 水曜日10時に変更されました。

questions.explanationZh: 文中要求 bring your updated sales numbers。
questions.explanationJa: updated sales numbers を持参します。

reading.passage: Dear Customer, Your package was delivered to the front desk at 2:15 p.m. Please show your ID card when you pick it up. The front desk is open until 8 p.m. today.

questions.explanationZh: package was delivered to the front desk。
questions.explanationJa: front desk に届けられました。

questions.explanationZh: pick it up 時需要 show your ID card。

reading.passage: To all employees, Registration for the safety training session closes this Friday. The session will be held next Tuesday in the main conference room. Attendance is required for new staff.

questions.explanationJa: 登録締切が金曜日です。

questions.explanationZh: Attendance is required for new staff。
questions.explanationJa: 新しいスタッフは参加必須です。

reading.passage: Dear Ms. Lee, I attached the revised contract for your review. If everything looks correct, please sign it and return it by the end of the week. Let me know if you have any questions.

questions.prompt: What is attached to the email?
questions.explanationZh: 開頭寫 I attached the revised contract。
questions.explanationJa: 修正版の契約書が添付されています。

questions.prompt: What is Ms. Lee asked to do?
questions.explanationZh: 請她 sign it and return it。

reading.passage: Notice: The service desk will open one hour later than usual on Monday because of staff training. Normal hours will resume on Tuesday. Customers with urgent issues should use the online request form.

questions.prompt: Why will the service desk open late?
questions.explanationZh: 文章寫 because of staff training。
questions.explanationJa: スタッフ研修のためです。

questions.prompt: What should customers with urgent issues use?
questions.explanationZh: urgent issues should use the online request form。
questions.explanationJa: オンライン依頼フォームを使います。
